bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started (t=0.0). The opponent proposed an offer consisting entirely of `valueE`, which provides the absolute minimum utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and set clear expectations, I will start by proposing my ideal outcome: `valueA` for all issues, targeting my maximum possible utility of 1.00.

The negotiation is very early (t = 0.02). The opponent has offered their apparent maximum twice in a row, which is my absolute minimum (valueE across all issues). As a gesture of good faith to get the negotiation moving without giving up significant value, I will make a small concession on one of my least important issues (issueE) from my optimal preference to the next best, while holding firm on the highest-weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The current time is very early (t=0.04), and the opponent is completely holding firm on their opening offer of all 'valueE's, which provides me the lowest possible utility. I will continue to make small concessions on my least important issues (issueD and issueE) to encourage the opponent to move, while keeping my most heavily weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) at their maximum value. I will slightly adjust issueD down to 'valueB'.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their initial offer, which provides me with the lowest possible utility (valueE on all issues). Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), there is no need to make rushed concessions. I will continue making small concessions on my least important issues to show a willingness to negotiate, dropping my demand on issueE from valueB to valueC, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent is holding firm on their initial opening offer, which represents my lowest possible utility (all `valueE`). Since we are in the very early stage of the negotiation (t=0.08), I will slowly concede on the least important issues (issueD and issueE, each with 0.1 importance) while holding firm on the highest importance issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to see if the opponent is willing to make a move.

The opponent is repeating their opening offer which provides minimal utility to me, making no concessions so far. Since the time is still very early (t=0.1), I will maintain my positions on my most valuable issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) but offer another small concession on a less critical issue (issueE) to signal my willingness to reach an agreement and test if they have any flexibility whatsoever.

The opponent has repeatedly offered the same bid since the negotiation started, showing no willingness to compromise so far. We are still in the very early stage of the negotiation (t=0.12), so there is no need to make large concessions. I will make a minor concession by lowering the value of my lowest-weighted issues (issueD and issueE) to signal flexibility while firmly defending my high-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent has rigidly maintained their opening offer of 'valueE' for all issues despite my repeated concessions on the lower-weighted issues. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will concede entirely on my lowest priority issue (issueE) to match their desired value exactly, hoping this gesture breaks their stubbornness. My utility remains high (0.86) as I hold firm on the high-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

I am at time fraction 0.16. The opponent has repeatedly offered their most extreme bid which gives me minimal utility, showing absolutely no willingness to compromise so far. To encourage movement while protecting my interests, I will incrementally concede on my moderately important issue (issueC) to valueB, after having made several concessions on the less important issues (issueD and issueE), maintaining high utility in my top issues.

The opponent remains entirely inflexible, repeating their opening offer on every turn. Since `time_fraction` is 0.18, it is still early enough to continue a slow but steady paced concession mostly on low-weight issues. Given the opponent's insistence on `valueE` across the board, I will fully concede `issueD` and `issueE` to their requested value and partially concede `issueC` to `valueB`, while maintaining my top preferences on my most important issues `issueA` and `issueB` (yielding an overall utility of 0.80).

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all 'valueE', showing no flexibility at all. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.20), I will continue to make small, gradual concessions downwards to signal a willingness to find a compromise, dropping my demands on issueC slightly while keeping my top priorities, issueA and issueB, at my most preferred values.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating the exact same bid, which represents the lowest possible utility for me across all issues. Given that it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), I will not capitulate. To encourage the opponent to move and reveal their priorities, I will make a lateral concession by lowering issueB to valueB and raising issueC back to valueB, keeping my utility around 0.74 while offering a different combination.

The opponent continues to hold firm with their absolute best (and my absolute worst) offer, making no concessions so far. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will maintain a high-utility posture while slightly adjusting my offers to find potential tradeoffs. I will hold strong on my most heavily weighted issues while showing moderate flexibility on the minor ones.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their extreme opening bid, giving me the lowest possible utility, and not conceding an inch despite my gradual concessions on lower-weight issues (D and E). Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold my ground on my highest-weight issues (A and B) while presenting a slightly different package on the remaining issues to encourage the opponent to move.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (yielding me only 0.20 overall utility) since the beginning of the negotiation. Given that the time fraction is still early (t=0.28), I will stop conceding rapidly and hold my ground on the most important issues (issueA and issueB), while offering minor concessions on issueE and issueD to see if prioritizing specific trade-offs will prompt any movement from their side.

The opponent is completely holding firm on their initial offer across all issues. Given that we are at t=0.30, I will start exploring trades involving my most important issues to uncover the opponent's true priorities. I am now offering a concession on issueA (valueB) while returning issueB back to valueA, maintaining a strong overall utility around 0.74 for myself while showing flexibility in new areas.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (all valueE) since the start, showing no willingness to concede. As the time fraction is 0.32, it is still early, so I will maintain a relatively high utility for myself (~0.72) while offering slightly different combinations of values on the lower-weighted issues. This allows me to explore which dimensions the opponent might actually value more while keeping my core interests protected.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer across all issues, maintaining valueE for every issue since the start. At t=0.34, I am still in the early-to-mid stage and should not make drastic concessions, particularly on my highly weighted issues (issueA and issueB). I will maintain my highest possible utility on my most important issues while offering maximum possible concessions (valueE and valueD) on my lower-weighted issues (issueD, issueE, and issueC) to see if they will reciprocate.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, continually offering 'valueE' across the board without any sign of concession. To probe their preferences while protecting my overall utility, I am testing different permutations of concessions. For this offer, I will concede slightly on issueA while keeping my top priorities on issueB and issueC, maintaining maximum concessions on my lowest priority issues (D and E) to target an overall utility of 0.72.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on an offer that provides me with minimum utility (all valueE). Since we are nearing the mid-game (t=0.38) and the opponent refuses to budge, I will explore a new combination by slightly conceding on my two highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) while maintaining high utility on issueC, and fully granting them valueE on the lower-weight issues (issueD and issueE). This signals flexibility without sacrificing too much core utility.

The opponent has rigidly maintained their opening bid of minimizing my utility (conceding nothing) up to t=0.40. While I have made numerous reasonable concessions primarily on my lower-weighted issues (D and E) to find a tradeoff, there has been no reciprocation. Given that we are still in the mid-game, I will hold my overall utility target around 0.70, offering a slightly different package by reducing issueC while holding firm on my most important issues, hoping the opponent will finally engage.

The opponent is remaining completely inflexible, offering their same maximum-utility bid for every turn without making any concessions. As we are approaching the midway point (t=0.42), I will hold my utility at around 0.68-0.70 to signal that I expect them to start negotiating. I am maintaining my high-importance issues (A, B, C) at competitive values while staying fully conceded on the lower-importance ones (D, E) in an attempt to prompt a counter-offer.

The opponent has repeatedly insisted on 'valueE' for all issues without any concessions, establishing a very firm stance. As we approach the mid-game (t=0.44), I will continue to signal cooperation by fully conceding on my lower-weight issues (issueD and issueE) to 'valueE'. I will also test asymmetrical combinations on my higher-weight issues, aiming for a utility around 0.68, to find a compromise that might break their deadlock.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the exact same offer (`valueE` for all issues) without making any concessions since the start of the negotiation. To try and break the deadlock while protecting my core utility, I will continue to offer `valueE` on my two least important issues (D and E). I will also gradually concede on my moderately important issue C and split the difference on my highest-weight issues A and B, targeting a total utility of around 0.64.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (`valueE` for all issues) throughout the negotiation. Since the deadline is roughly halfway (t=0.48), I will now explicitly offer `valueE` on the three issues I care about least (issues C, D, and E) to see if giving them their top choice on a majority of issues breaks their stubbornness. This allows me to hold firm on my top two priorities (issues A and B) and maintains a solid total utility of 0.68 for myself.

The opponent has finally shown a willingness to concede by moving `issueA` to `valueD` at the halfway mark (`t=0.50`). To encourage further concessions, I will maintain my high-preference issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) near the top of their value range, while offering moderate-to-high concessions on lower-weight issues (`issueC`, `issueD`, `issueE`) to find mutually acceptable ground.

The opponent is remaining extremely firm, consistently bidding their ideal outcome (valueE on all issues) and reverting immediately after a minuscule concession on issueA. We are around the midway point (t=0.52), so I will continue to hold firm on my most highly valued issues (issueA and issueB) while maintaining full concessions on my lowest-priority issues (issueD and issueE). This offer proposes mid-tier options for issueB and issueC, targeting a utility of approximately 0.64 without needlessly surrendering more value to an unyielding party.

The opponent primarily demands `valueE` for all issues but made a slight concession on `issueA` (`valueD`) earlier when I offered `valueE` for issues C, D, and E. Since we are just past the midpoint (t=0.54), I will propose a moderate concession. By giving the opponent their preferred `valueE` exactly for issues C, D, and E, I will lower my demands slightly on `issueA` while keeping `issueB` at maximum utility, targeting an overall acceptable utility of ~0.62.

The opponent has stubbornly continued proposing 'valueE' for all issues, with only a minor deviation at t=0.50, demonstrating a rigid preference for that value. As we are barely past the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.56), I will continue accommodating their preference for 'valueE' on my least important issues (D and E), while varying offers on my higher-weight issues (A, B, and C) to discover a combination they might accept, maintaining my utility above 0.65. Specifically, I will accept 'valueE' on issues D and E and request a mix of top-tier returns on A, B, and C.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating an offer of `valueE` for all issues, with only a minor deviation to `issueA: valueD` at t=0.50. I will fully concede on my low-weight issues D and E, matching their requested `valueE`, to test if they will reciprocate. In exchange, I will keep values on my higher-weight issues (A, B, C) relatively high to target a moderate utility of around 0.66 mid-game.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, repeating the exact same base offer in almost every turn, showing very little intent to compromise. Since the deadline is approaching (t=0.6), I need to propose a reasonable combination that compromises on my most valued issues (like issueA) while securing strong value in others. I am offering a mix that retains a decent utility (0.68) while exploring value variations that the opponent might be willing to accept.

The opponent has marginally conceded on `issueA` by proposing `valueD` instead of `valueE`. To encourage momentum, I will match this concession by accepting `valueD` for `issueA`, and I will continue offering their preferred `valueE` for my low-importance issues (`issueD` and `issueE`). To ensure my utility remains reasonable at this stage (~0.66), I will hold firm on `valueA` for `issueB` and `issueC`.

The opponent is holding very firm with values mostly at 'valueE', but their latest offer explored 'valueD' for issueB. Since I want to encourage them to make concessions and continue exploring this space, I will adopt 'valueD' for issueB while keeping my most important issues at higher values. This maintains my own utility at a reasonable 0.66 for this mid-stage of the negotiation, matching the utility level of my recent offers while reciprocating their exact variation.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on issues C, D, and E, almost entirely insisting on `valueE`, while showing tiny flexibility on `issueA` and `issueB`. Knowing that `issueD` and `issueE` are my lowest-weight preferences, I will continue conceding those entirely to `valueE`. I will offer a balanced middle-ground for my high priority issues `issueA` and `issueB` (setting them both to `valueB`) while also making a slight concession on `issueC` to accommodate their persistence without dropping my overall utility too sharply.

The opponent has made minor concessions to `valueD` on either `issueA` or `issueB` recently. Since my utility is highest for `issueA` and `issueB`, I will continue conceding on my least important issues (`issueD` and `issueE`) down to `valueE` where the opponent is holding firm. I will offer a moderate compromise on `issueA` and `issueB` while maintaining a utility around 0.66, testing the opponent's flexibility on the top issues.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueE` on most issues, but has recently signaled minor concessions on `issueA` and `issueB` by sometimes proposing `valueD` for them. To find common ground while protecting my utility at `t=0.7`, I will continue to fully concede on my lowest-weighted issues (`issueD`, `issueE`), and offer a middle-ground combination on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC` aiming for a utility around 0.62. This distributes concessions across the higher-weight issues without dropping my utility too quickly.

The opponent has remained quite firm, predominantly asking for 'valueE' across all issues, but has recently shown willingness to concede to 'valueD' on issueC, issueA, and issueB in separate offers. Given that time is at `t=0.72`, I will incorporate their latest concession on issueC (valueD) while aiming for a utility of around 0.60 by claiming 'valueB' on my higher-weight issues A and B, and fully conceding on issues D and E.

The opponent significantly conceded on my most important issue, `issueA`, by offering `valueA` in their last bid. Since time is advancing (t=0.74), I will accept their generous offer on `issueA` while fully conceding to their preferred `valueE` on the lower-weighted issues (`issueC`, `issueD`, and `issueE`). I will retain `valueB` on `issueB` to maintain a strong overall utility (~0.62), testing if they can accept this tradeoff to finalize the agreement.

The opponent has made a significant concession by offering valueA for my most important issue (issueA). To reciprocate while protecting my utility, I will lock in issueA at valueA and offer a compromise on the mid-weighted issues (issueB and issueC) by moving to valueC, while keeping issueD and issueE at their preferred valueE. This maintains an overall utility of 0.64 for me and uses the remaining time wisely before reaching my bottom line.

Late in the negotiation at t=0.78, the opponent has shown a willingness to concede issueA to valueA (at t=0.74) and more recently issueC to valueD (at t=0.78). I will incorporate these concessions to form a mutually beneficial package, keeping my low-weight issues (D and E) at their preferred valueE. I will offer valueC on issueB to compromise, maintaining an overall utility of 0.60 for myself.

The time is currently at t=0.80. The opponent has recently conceded highly on issueA and somewhat on issueB and issueC, indicating these are lower priority for them compared to issueD and issueE. I will hold firm on issueA=valueA and fully concede on issueD and issueE to valueE, while proposing a compromise on issueB and issueC to maintain my utility around 0.58.

We are entering the late stage of the negotiation (t=0.82). The opponent has shown willingness to concede on issueA (up to valueA at t=0.76), issueB (up to valueD at t=0.78 and 0.80), and issueC (up to valueD at t=0.78). I will propose a composite offer that grants the opponent valueE on the lowest-weighted issues (issueD and issueE) while combining their best historical concessions on issueA, issueB, and issueC in order to reach an agreement that still provides me with a utility above 0.5.

We are late in the negotiation (t=0.84). By analyzing the opponent's offer history, I can see they are willing to concede up to 'valueA' on issueA (at t=0.74) and 'valueB' on issueB (at t=0.84). Since these are my highest-weighted issues, I will propose a combination that locks in these previous concessions while completely conceding to 'valueE' on issueD and issueE, which the opponent has steadfastly refused to change. This maintains a strong utility of 0.70 for me while directly addressing their revealed constraints.

The current time fraction is 0.86, indicating we are in the late stages of negotiation. The opponent has started making minor concessions on my high-priority issues, such as proposing `valueA` and `valueB` for `issueA`, and `valueB` for `issueB` on alternating turns. I will propose an offer that captures strong utility on my two highest value issues (A and B) while meeting their consistent preference for `valueE` on issueC and issueE, while offering `valueD` for issueD, reflecting a value they introduced in their last turn.

The time fraction is 0.88, which means the deadline is approaching, so I need to find mutually acceptable compromises. The opponent's last offer suggested a willingness to accept `valueA` on `issueA` and `valueD` on `issueC` and `issueD`. I will propose a bid that embraces these values while returning `issueB` to `valueB`, ensuring a utility for me around 0.68, maintaining momentum toward an agreement.

The negotiation is in its late stages (t=0.90), so I need to make significant concessions to secure an agreement, targeting a utility of around 0.60-0.65. The opponent recently offered `issueB: valueC`, `issueC: valueD`, and `issueD: valueD`, as well as `issueA: valueA` in prior rounds. I will assemble a bid that comprises their acceptable values for the minor issues while securing my most preferred value for `issueA`.

The time fraction is 0.92, so we are approaching the deadline. I must make a strong compromise. The opponent has previously shown willingness to accept 'valueA' for issueA (at t=0.88) and 'valueC' for issueB (at t=0.90), as well as 'valueD' for issueC. I will combine these past concessions into a single offer while fully yielding to 'valueE' on my lower-priority issues (issueD and issueE) to secure an agreement before time runs out.
